summary: dstack hardening / pin-set. dstack is packaging + transport + an optional KMS, never the sole root of trust. These pins MUST hold before any high-value key is rooted in a dstack-managed CVM.

pinnedRelease.note: Owner decision (plan §8.3): we intentionally TRACK THE LATEST dstack release (>= the Feb-2026 'Secure-by-Default' attestation-pipeline baseline) rather than freezing a tag, so upstream hardening fixes land automatically. Trust is NOT rooted in the dstack version but in the platform RoT + our signed golden measurements (see rootOfTrust). EVERY boot independently re-verifies QE-identity, TCB-status, the golden measurements, and the forbid/require invariants, so a malicious or downgraded release still cannot release keys (reverifyOnUpdate=true).

appAuthAllowlist.note: On-chain AppAuth allowlist of accepted agent/container/compose code hashes. DERIVED FROM the signed golden manifest: these MUST equal release/confidential-2026-05-21/manifest.json tee.measurements.agent, .container and .compose. When the real reproducible image lands, the manifest and this allowlist update together. An empty allowlist FAILS CLOSED (no code hash is trusted).

rootOfTrust.note: TDX module / E1 RoT plus our signed golden tee-measurements.json. dstack-KMS is never the sole anchor; on-device verifier (eliza-local-verifier) is the default, Eliza Cloud KMS optional.

openIssues.summary: dstack upstream hardening tracking issue — see tee-measured-boot-contract.md 'dstack Hardening Pins'.
